phonetic: 藢su藧p蓹藢f瑟蕛i藞忙l蓹ti; 藢sju藧p蓹藢f瑟蕛i藞忙l蓹ti
meaning: n.琛ㄩ潰鎬х殑浜嬬墿,娴呰杽
examples: The same superficiality is found in nearly all kinds of business.
There is also a feeling of superficiality about much online youth activism.
We had the chance of scoring, but we also paid for some superficiality.
Unequal wealth, of course, loses its superficiality to the degree that it defines politics in a democracy.
When painting royals and nobility, Zoffany only occasionally got beyond the superficiality of his subjects' clothes and settings.
